title: Liberia's iron ore ramp-up aims to boost Capesize demand in 2026
section_path: Liberia's iron ore ramp-up aims to boost Capesize demand in 2026
section_path_text: Liberia's iron ore ramp-up aims to boost Capesize demand in 2026
summary:
Main
Discussions on Capesize trade growth opportunities from West Africa have focused on Guinea, thanks to soaring bauxite exports and the long-awaited start of iron ore operations from the Simandou project, from where five ore shipments have been recorded to date. This week newswires reported an ambitious claim from Liberia's mines minister that the country's iron ore exports will triple from 10m tonnes in 2025. There is an added dimension to the expansion as Capesize vessels became involved in Liberian iron ore trades during the 2H 2025 and are set for greater involvement this year, effectively creating a new Capesize trade.
